text:
Bulkers: VV Bulker values remained mostly stable across all bulker sectors. Newcastlemax and Capesize values drifted marginallylower while Handysize saw positive movement especially for resales.
Panamax Mont Blanc Hawk (81,600 DWT, 2017, Imabari) sold by Eiko Kisen for USD 32.8 mil, VV Value USD 31.1 mil.
Panamax Marinicki C (76,600 DWT, 2005, Imabari) sold by Chandris to Chinese buyers for USD 11.5 mil, VV Value USD 10.4 mil.
Supramax African Tern (58,300 DWT, 2013, Dalian COSCO KHI) sold by MUR Shipping for USD 19.8 mil, VV Value USD 20.1 mil.
Supramax Santa Rita (55,700 DWT, 2010, Mitsui Tamano) sold by Okouchi Kaiun to Greek buyers for USD 17.2 mil, VV Value USD 17.5 mil.
Handy BC Dalarna (34,800 DWT, Jul 2014, Zhong Chuan) sold by Bertling FH for USD 17.2 mil, VV Value USD 14.0 mil.
Tankers: VV S&P values were broadly stable on a one-week basis. Modern Aframax values saw slight softening while older tonnage continues to appreciate.
Suezmax Cosmo Sail (159,200 DWT, 2007, Hyundai Samho) sold by Taihu Shipping for USD 49.5 mil, VV Value USD 48.6 mil.
LR2 Seriana (110,000 DWT, Sep 2015, Sumitomo) sold to Great Eastern Shipping for USD 72.0 mil, VV Value USD 73.0 mil.
LR1 Cape Taura & Cape Tempest (73,600-73,700 DWT, 2007/2008, New Times Shipbuilding) sold en bloc to Perosea Shipping for USD 20.5 mil and USD 22.1 mil respectively, VV Values USD 20.5 mil and USD 21.2 mil.
MR2 Sandpiper Pacific (51,800 DWT, Jun 2013, HSG Sungdong Shipbuilding) sold by Pacific Carriers (Singapore) to Undisclosed for

MR2 Sandpiper Pacific (51,800 DWT, Jun 2013, HSG Sungdong Shipbuilding) sold by Pacific Carriers (Singapore) to Undisclosed for USD 32.0 mil, VV Value USD 31.7 mil.
Containers: No new sales this week, but demand remains high, with values still coming up for older Feedermaxes.
No notable sales

Bulkers: Panamax values rose in response to high prices achieved by last week's Mont Blanc Hawk and Marinicki C sales.Supramax values dipped in response to the soft pricing of the Ocean Bright.
Supramax Ocean Bright (56,000 DWT, 2013, Mitsui) sold by Ming Tai Shipping Corp DD Passed for USD 19.2 mil, VV Value USD 21.0 mil.
Handy Sun Grace (33,700 DWT, 2004, Oshima) sold by STX Sun Ace Shipping to Greek buyers for USD 7.25 mil, VV Value USD 8.0 mil.
Tankers: VV S&P values saw positive movement across older tonnage this week, with older VLCCs, Suezmaxes and MR2s all appreciating. Older MR2 values being the standout performer. Asset values for older VLCCs remain well supported, with the Lila Kochi sale landing against a backdrop of tight fleet supply and healthy tanker earnings.
VLCC Lila Kochi (314,000 DWT, Dec 2008, Kawasaki) sold to MSC for USD 79.0 mil, VV Value USD 74.01 mil.
VLCC Yamatogawa (302,500 DWT, Dec 2006, Kawasaki) sold SS/DD Due by Chiba Shipping to Chinese buyers for USD 61.5 mil, VV Value USD 62.33 mil.
Containers: The S&P market remains quiet and demand remains high, values are still nudging up for older Feedermaxes but no notable movements across any other sectors.
Feedermax Steen (801 TEU, 2008, Peters SB) sold DD due to Turkish Buyers for USD 10 mil, VV Value USD 10.3 mil.

Bulkers: Midage Supramax values dipped in response to soft pricing for the Livita,while older tonnage firmed in response to the sale of the VW Trust to Chinese buyers.
Kamsarmax AC Youth (82,600 DWT, 2007, Tadotsu Tsuneishi) sold by Glorious Youth Shipping for USD 15 mil, VV Value USD 14.3 mil.
Ultramax Livita (63,500 DWT, 2017, Shin Kasado Dock) sold to Greek buyers for USD 30.5 mil, VV Value USD 30.6 mil.
Supramax VW Trust (52,500 DWT, 2002, Tadotsu Tsuneishi) sold to Chinese buyers for USD 8 mil, VV Value USD 7.8 mil.
Handy Astro Propus (38,300 DWT, 2014, Imabari) sold to Devbulk Shipping for USD 19 mil, VV Value USD 19.1 mil.
Tankers: VV S&P values were broadly stable on a one-week basis, with MR values ticking up modestly across the board. Sentiment continues to be supported by easing Middle East tensions and continued growth in U.S. crude exports
VLCC Eclat (299,000 DWT, 2004, Universal) sold to All Win Ship Management for USD 50 mil, VV Value USD 51.78 mil.
LR2 Jag Lokesh (105,900 DWT, 2009, Hyundai Heavy Ind Ulsan Shipyard) sold by Great Eastern Shipping to Y-Knot Ship Management for USD 44.10 mil, VV Value USD 48.60 mil.
MR2 Dylan (50,300 DWT, 2009, Guangzhou Shipyard International) sold by Kurdran Shipping to undisclosed buyers for USD 20.90 mil, VV Value USD 19.12 mil.
MR2 Lanikai (46,300 DWT, 2002, STX Offshore) sold by Lanikai Shipping to undisclosed Chinese buyers for USD 9.50 mil, VV Value USD 10.14 mil.
MR2 Caroline (45,000 DWT, 2002, STX Offshore) sold by Caroline Shipping to undisclosed Chinese buyers for USD 9.50 mil, VV Value USD 9.91 mil.

MR2 Lanikai (46,300 DWT, 2002, STX Offshore) sold by Lanikai Shipping to undisclosed Chinese buyers for USD 9.50 mil, VV Value USD 10.14 mil.
MR2 Caroline (45,000 DWT, 2002, STX Offshore) sold by Caroline Shipping to undisclosed Chinese buyers for USD 9.50 mil, VV Value USD 9.91 mil.
Containers: A stable story across container values this week with little movement across most sectors, older Feedermaxes continue their upward trend. Port congestion and no notable changes in the Strait of Hormuz keeping vessel utilisation high.
Sub Panamax AS Angelina (2,127 TEU, 2007, Wadan Wards MTW) sold with forward delivery by MPC Container Ships for USD 17 mil, VV Value USD 22.5 mil.
Handy Container AS Selina (1,740 TEU, 2012, Guangzhou Wenchong) sold with forward delivery by MPC Container Ships for USD 24 mil, VV Value USD 24 mil.

Bulkers: Value movements were subdued this week, as achieved prices held broadly steady,with the exception of a slight correction in mid-age Supramax vessels.
Kamsarmax Polynesia Queen (82,200 DWT, 2012, Tsuneishi Zhoushan) sold by Laus Ocean Shipping for USD 21 mil, VV Value USD 21.1 mil.
Ultramax Livita (63,500 DWT, 2017, Shin Kasado Dock) sold by Ugland Bulk Shipping AS for USD 30.5 mil, VV Value USD 30.9 mil.
Supramax Sea Abigail (53,300 DWT, 2003, Kanasashi) sold by Sea Castle Ship Management to UAE buyers for USD 8.8 mil, VV Value USD 8.7 mil.
Handy HTK Lucky (28,500 DWT, 2003, Imabari) sold by HTK Shipping & Import Export to Chinese buyers for USD 6 mil, VV Value USD 5.6 mil.
Tankers: The Tanker S&P market was quiet this week, with mid-age MR values continuing to correct as a result of previous sales.
No notable sales this week.
Containers: Feedermax values continued to move this week with two notable sales, seemingly the only segment where charter-free tonnage is still available.
Feedermax Alexander B (1,221 TEU, 2006, Detlef Hegemann Rolandwerft) sold to undisclosed buyers for USD 11.5 mil, VV Value USD 11 mil.
Feedermax A Goryu (1,096 TEU, 2023, Kyokuyo) sold to Chinese buyers for USD 26.5 mil, VV Value USD 27 mil.
